summary.intent: Decide whether to lower the ars-components branch coverage threshold in CI, which was failing at 90% despite the actual coverage being only marginally below that value.
summary.outcome: Lowered the ars-components branch coverage threshold from 90% to 89% in xtask/src/coverage.rs, committed and pushed. The exact CI LCOV artifact was verified to pass at the new threshold.

repo: ars-components branch coverage threshold is defined in xtask/src/coverage.rs and is separate per-crate, not a single global value
The CI coverage gate compares unrounded floating-point values, but the display table rounds to one decimal — so a value like 89.98% rounds to 90.0% in the table but still fails the 90% gate
Coverage diagnostics are uploaded as a GitHub Actions artifact named 'coverage-diagnostics' containing an lcov.info file, downloadable via 'gh run download'

ars-components is a large aggregate crate where each new component adds defensive state-machine branches before component-specific tests exist, making crate-wide branch coverage thresholds inherently volatile

code.finding: Per-package coverage thresholds (line and branch minimums) are defined here; ars-components branch min was changed from 90 to 89

workflow: Download the CI LCOV artifact locally and run 'cargo xtask coverage check' against it with proposed new thresholds before committing, to confirm the change actually fixes the failure
Use a perl one-liner against lcov.info to get per-file branch hit/total counts for precise diagnosis when the CI table only shows rounded aggregates
Run 'cargo test -p xtask coverage::tests' to validate threshold logic unit tests before pushing a coverage policy change

friction: The GitHub Actions log inspection script hit the wrong tail of the log, requiring a manual sed range extraction to find the actual threshold failure lines
gh artifact download required multiple intermediate tool calls (listing artifacts, downloading, finding the file path) before the lcov.info path was known
The CI table displays rounded coverage (90.0%) which masks that the failure is a floating-point rounding edge case, making the root cause non-obvious from the UI alone
open_items: Revisit ars-components branch coverage threshold once the data-display/input/selection component catalog stabilizes and more component-specific tests exist to smooth the denominator
Consider whether the coverage gate should use rounded values for comparison to avoid rounding-edge false failures in the future
